ddls:
CREATE TABLE "commits" (
  "commit_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each commit. */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the commit in the version control system. */,
  "date" TEXT /* Date when the commit was made. */,
  "title" TEXT /* Title or summary of the commit. */,
  "changes" INTEGER /* Number of changes made in the commit. */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file that was modified. */,
  "reason_for_consistency" TEXT /* Reason for maintaining consistency in commits. */,
  "is_trivial" INTEGER /* Flag indicating if the commit is trivial or non-trivial. */,
  "old_hunk_file" TEXT /* Path to the old version of the file. */,
  "new_hunk_file" TEXT /* Path to the new version of the file. */,
  "old_loc" INTEGER /* Old line number affected by the change. */,
  "new_loc" INTEGER /* New line number affected by the change. */,
  "old_log" TEXT /* Log message from the old version. */,
  "new_log" TEXT /* Log message from the new version. */,
  "edit_types" TEXT /* Types of edits made in the commit. */,
  "edit_words" TEXT /* Words that were edited in the commit. */,
  "edit_feature" TEXT /* Feature or functionality affected by the commit. */,
  "cluster" INTEGER /* Cluster ID associated with the commit. */,
  "wait_time" REAL /* Wait time associated with the commit processing. */,
  "cluster_size" INTEGER /* Size of the cluster involved in the commit. */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who made the commit. */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user who reviewed the commit. */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the commit (e.g., pending, approved, rejected). */,
  "branch" TEXT /* Name of the branch where the commit was made. */,
  "repository_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the repository where the commit was made. */,
  "merge_request_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the merge request associated with the commit. */,
  "ci_cd_status" TEXT /* Status of the CI/CD pipeline (e.g., success, failure, running). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("commit_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_commits_reviewer_id FOREIGN KEY ("reviewer_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_commits_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "files" (
  "file_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each file entry. */,
  "commit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the commit associated with this file. */,
  "file_name" TEXT /* Name of the file that was modified. */,
  "old_hunk_file" TEXT /* Path to the old version of the file. */,
  "new_hunk_file" TEXT /* Path to the new version of the file. */,
  "repository_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the repository where the file is located. */,
  "branch" TEXT /* Name of the branch where the file was modified. */,
  "file_size" INTEGER /* Size of the file in bytes. */,
  "file_type" TEXT /* Type of the file (e.g., .cpp, .h, .py). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("file_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_files_commit_id FOREIGN KEY ("commit_id") REFERENCES commits ("commit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "edit_types" (
  "edit_type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each edit type. */,
  "edit_type_name" TEXT /* Name of the edit type (e.g., updateLog, removeLog). */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of what the edit type entails. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("edit_type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "clusters" (
  "cluster_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each cluster. */,
  "size" INTEGER /* Size of the cluster associated with commits. */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the cluster. */,
  "repository_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the repository associated with the cluster. */,
  "branch" TEXT /* Name of the branch associated with the cluster. */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the cluster (e.g., active, inactive). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("cluster_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user. */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user. */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user. */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., developer, reviewer). */,
  "team_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the team the user belongs to. */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department of the user (e.g., development, QA). */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Access level of the user (e.g., read, write, admin). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry. */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the user accessing the commit. */,
  "commit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the commit being accessed. */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed by the user (e.g., view, edit). */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the action occurred. */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the user. */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string of the user. */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the action (e.g., success, failure). */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_commit_id FOREIGN KEY ("commit_id") REFERENCES commits ("commit_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "commit_metadata" (
  "metadata_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each metadata entry. */,
  "commit_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the associated commit. */,
  "key" TEXT /* Key for the metadata item. */,
  "value" TEXT /* Value corresponding to the key. */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("metadata_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_commit_metadata_commit_id FOREIGN KEY ("commit_id") REFERENCES commits ("commit_id")
)
